title: The First Wish
description: How this world began: a wish to make learning visible.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: Before there is a world, there is a wish.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: I wish learning could become something students can see and enter.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: Then the first rule is simple: knowledge needs a form.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: The first page was small, but the wish inside it was not.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: But one page could not carry every piece of knowledge.

projectStage.title: Prototype v0: Question Only
projectStage.caption: A recovered fragment from the time when the world was only one question and one answer.

title: The First Towers
description: Why knowledge was divided into towers and smaller challenges.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: So we raised the First Towers.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: Each tower held one kind of knowledge.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: Then divide the great knowledge into smaller challenges.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: Fractions stood with fractions, and shapes stood with shapes.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: The Towers protected knowledge... but knowledge alone could teach no one.

projectStage.title: Prototype v1: The First Towers
projectStage.caption: A recovered fragment from the time when large knowledge was divided into smaller tower challenges.

title: The First Questions
description: Why questions became the foundation of learning in this world.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: So we created the First Questions.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: A tower could not be restored by battle alone.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: A question asks the traveler to think, choose, and learn.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: The world would grow through answers, not combat.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: The Questions allowed the world to grow... but growth must also be remembered.

projectStage.title: Prototype v2: The First Questions
projectStage.caption: A living fragment from the time when questions became the heart of the towers.

title: The First Memory
description: Why the world can remember what has been restored.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: So we taught the world to remember.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: A world that forgets cannot grow.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: Each answer, each tower, and each fragment must be remembered.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: A save is not magic. It is a record of what has been restored.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: The world began to keep records of restored progress.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: Students would see that memory is structure.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: Memory alone was not enough.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: Sometimes a path must be walked again.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: So another rule was needed.

projectStage.title: Prototype v3: The First Memory
projectStage.caption: A recovered fragment from the time when the world learned to remember restored progress.

title: The Remembered Path
description: Why loading means returning to a path the world already remembers.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: So we created the Remembered Paths.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: Yes. A load does not create a new world.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: It returns the traveler to a remembered path.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: Names, fragments, records, and positions could come back together.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: At last... the world was ready.

projectStage.title: Prototype v4: The Remembered Path
projectStage.caption: A recovered fragment from the time when loading returned travelers to a remembered path.

title: The Completed World
description: How T and G completed the world the player has now recovered.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: And together... we completed the world.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: The wish had a form.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: The towers stood, the questions taught, and the records awakened.

dialogue.speaker: G
dialogue.text: The world was complete.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: But no one had yet walked its paths.

dialogue.speaker: T
dialogue.text: At the center, the final trial waited.

projectStage.title: Prototype v5: The Complete Prototype
projectStage.caption: A recovered fragment from the current era. The first world is complete, and the next creator is almost ready.
